hot-humid (90 degrees C, 60% RH) aging on cell/encapsulant/glass coupons.", "direct_quote": "decreases in adhesion energy, Gc, from 5000 h of hot-dry (90 degrees C, ~1% RH) and hot-humid (90 degrees coupons demonstrated the best adhesion stability, followed by EPE composites and then EVA.", "direct_quote": "POE coupons demonstrated the best stability, followed by EPE then EVA", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.92, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["POE", "EPE", "EVA"]}, {"statement": "Hot-humid aged EVA and POE coupons experienced larger Gc decrease than hot-dry due to enhanced hydrolytic degradation.", "direct_quote": "hot-humid aged coupons experienced a larger decrease in Gc due to enhanced hydrolytic degradation", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.93, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["hydrolytic degradation", "Gc"]}, {"statement": "Gel content increased and crystallinity decreased under hot-aerobic (90 degrees C, 22% RH) aging even without UV or crosslinking initiators.", "direct_quote": "increase in gel content (crosslinking) and decrease in crystallinity of the encapsulants under hot-aerobic (90 degrees C, 22% RH)", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.88, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["gel content", "crosslinking", "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["FTIR-ATR", "UV", "moisture"]}], "evidence_summary": "NREL 2025 in IEEE JPHOTOV: EVA/POE/EPE at 90C/5000h. Humidity amplifies Gc loss via hydrolysis. POE most stable. UV+moisture critical degradation accelerators."}, {"source_url": "https://www.osti.gov/biblio/1579796", "source_title": "Adhesion and debonding kinetics of photovoltaic encapsulation in moist environments", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.8, "overall_relevance": 0.88, "year": 2016, "authors": ["Novoa F D", "Miller D C", "Dauskardt R H"], "venue": "Progress in Photovoltaics", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "EVA debond energy decreased from 2.15 to 1.75 kJ/m2 when temperature increased from 25 to 50 degrees C.", "direct_quote": "debond energy decreased from 2.15 to 1.75 kJ m-2 in poly(ethylene-co-vinyl acetate) (EVA)", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.93, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["EVA", "debond energy", "2.15 kJ/m2", "1.75 kJ/m2"]}, {"statement": "PVB debond energy decreased from 0.67 to 0.52 kJ/m2 when temperature increased from 20 to 40 degrees C.", "direct_quote": "from 0.67 to 0.52 kJ m-2 in polyvinyl butyral when T increased from 20 to 40 degrees C", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.78, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["PVB", "debond energy"]}, {"statement": "EVA debond growth rates increased up to 1000-fold with small increases of 10 degrees C and 15% RH.", "direct_quote": "debond growth rates of EVA increased up to 1000-fold with small increases of T (10 degrees C) and RH (15%)", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.9, "confidence": 0.95, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["debond growth rate", "EVA", "1000-fold"]}], "evidence_summary": "Stanford/NREL 2016 mechanics study: EVA Gc 2.15->1.75 kJ/m2 with 25C increase. Debond rate 1000x from +10C/+15%RH. Fracture-kinetics model for long-term prediction."}]}
